paper_title: Geochronology and Geological Evolution of Maio

methods.description: This method involves dating phlogopite crystals from dyke samples and a nephelinite lava clast using the 40 Ar-39 Ar technique to determine the geological age of formations.

steps.description: Samples were prepared for dating by crushing, sieving and washing in distilled water in an ultrasonic bath.
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare samples by crushing, sieving, and washing in ultrasonic bath

steps.description: Phlogopite separates in the 0.25-0.5 mm and 0.5-1 mm size fractions were separated and handpicked under a binocular microscope, and cleaned in acetone before drying in an oven at 50 °C overnight.
steps.searchable_summary: Separate and clean phlogopite crystals, then dry in oven

steps.description: The samples were loaded into an aluminum cannister, and irradiated with fast neutrons in a Cadmium-Lined In-Core Irradiation Tube (CLICIT) reactor for four hours at the Oregon State University nuclear reactor, Corvallis, USA.
steps.searchable_summary: Irradiate samples with fast neutrons in CLICIT reactor

steps.description: The samples were analyzed at the GEOMAR Argon Geochronology in Oceanography (ArGO) Laboratory in Kiel, Germany.
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze irradiated samples at ArGO Laboratory

steps.description: The isotopic analyses were corrected using the total K decay constant and atmospheric air ratio of Steiger and Jäger (1977); using the recommendation of Fleck et al. (2019), and J values were determined using the Taylor Creek sanidine (TCR-2) age standard.
steps.searchable_summary: Correct isotopic analyses using decay constants and age standards

methods.description: This method involves taking structural measurements and creating structural plots to analyze geological formations.

steps.description: Structural measurements were taken with the Lambert App, quoted with dip direction/dip angles.
steps.searchable_summary: Take structural measurements using Lambert App

steps.description: Structural plots were created with Stereonet v.11.
steps.searchable_summary: Create structural plots using Stereonet v.11
